Announcing

amplifier-bundle-
dot-graph

First-class DOT/Graphviz infrastructure for the entire Amplifier ecosystem

Phase 1 — Active

March 2026  ·  microsoft/amplifier-bundle-dot-graph

“The diagram didn’t document the bug.
Drawing the diagram found the bug.”

This is the story of how a diagramming format turned out to be an analysis tool — and why the Amplifier ecosystem now has a shared bundle for it.

The Problem

DOT everywhere.
Infrastructure nowhere.

🔄

Reinvented per project

Attractor pipelines, Parallax investigations, recipe visualizations, architecture docs, event specs — every project built DOT tooling from scratch.

🏝️

No shared knowledge

Hard-won DOT patterns, quality standards, and rendering tricks stayed locked inside individual sessions. No institutional memory.

🖼️

“Just pictures”

DOT was treated as a visualization afterthought. Nobody had articulated why DOT matters beyond producing diagrams — its analytical power was invisible.

The Discovery

Before writing any code,
we ran a massive research effort.

12+
Agent dispatches
across 3 waves
6,240
Sessions scanned
for DOT usage
70+
Existing DOT
artifacts cataloged
10
Research documents
produced

Multi-agent discovery: session mining, artifact cataloging, dialect comparison, ecosystem analysis, concept deep-dives. The research surfaced something unexpected.

The Insight

DOT is an
analysis tool.

Not just a diagramming format. Building a valid graph forces structural commitment.

🔬 Reconciliation Forcing Function

Every node must exist. Every edge must connect. The ambiguity that prose hides gets exposed by the graph’s formal structure. This surfaces bugs, dead code, gaps, and contradictions that LSP, compilers, and prose-based analysis routinely miss.

You think you understand the system — then you try to draw it. The diagram doesn’t just document what you know. It forces you to discover what you don’t.

Seven Use Cases

DOT serves the ecosystem
in seven distinct ways.

1. Reconciliation Forcing Function

Drawing the graph finds the bugs prose hides

2. Dense Context Representation

200 lines of DOT ≈ 2,000+ lines of prose

3. Multi-Scale Navigation

Subgraphs as zoom levels — Google Maps for code

4. Analysis Substrate

Parse → graph object → cycles, paths, impact. Zero tokens.

5. Multi-Modal Bridge

SVG/PNG for users, dashboards, and vision models

6. Workflow Visualization

Multi-step recipes become comprehensible flow graphs

7. Investigation Artifact

Forces structural claims — prevents vague analysis

Each use case is a first-class workflow, not a bolt-on.

Demo — Reconciliation

The naive view vs. what DOT actually found.

Before — “We understand this system”
After — 7 issues surfaced

Same system. The left is what we thought existed. The right is what DOT reconciliation actually found — dead code, missing edges, circular dependencies, and gaps the compiler never warned about.

Demo — Multi-Scale Navigation

Zoom out for context. Zoom in for detail.

Overview — Cluster level
Detail — Auth subsystem

Subgraphs act as zoom levels. The context window is the fixed “screen size” — DOT lets you navigate scale without losing orientation.

Phase 1 — Knowledge Layer

What shipped.

2
Agents
5
Skills
502
Tests
  • dot-author agent — creates & refines DOT (model_role: coding)
  • diagram-reviewer agent — PASS/WARN/FAIL verdicts (model_role: critique)
  • 5 skills: syntax, patterns, analysis, quality, graph-intelligence
  • 5 docs: syntax ref, patterns catalog, quality standards, graphviz setup, analysis guide
  • 2 context files: thin awareness pointer (~150 tokens) + mid-weight instructions
  • 1 composable behavior — one include line
  • 2 shell scripts: dot-validate.sh, dot-render.sh
  • Tool module skeleton ready for Phase 2
Integration

One line. Full DOT capabilities.

# In your bundle's behavior YAML: includes: - bundle: dot-graph:behaviors/dot-graph # That's it. You get: # dot-author agent (coding) # diagram-reviewer agent (critique) # 5 skills: syntax, patterns, analysis, quality, intelligence # DOT tools (Phase 2) # ~150 token awareness context

✅ Zero setup friction

Phase 1 has zero runtime dependencies. No Graphviz install required. The knowledge layer works immediately — agents, skills, and context are pure text infrastructure.

📦 Composable with any bundle

The behavior include pattern means any Amplifier bundle can gain DOT literacy in one line. Already used in Parallax, recipe visualization, and architecture tooling.

Architecture & Roadmap

Three-tier design.

T1

Knowledge Layer Shipped

Agents + context + skills. Zero runtime dependencies. Pure knowledge infrastructure that makes any Amplifier agent DOT-literate.

T2

Validation & Rendering Tools Next

pydot-based three-layer validation (syntax → structural → render-quality). Graphviz CLI wrapper for SVG/PNG/PDF output. Real tool module, not just knowledge.

T3

Graph Intelligence Next

NetworkX-backed analysis: reachability queries, cycle detection, critical path analysis, structural diffs between graph versions. Zero LLM tokens for structural questions.

Each tier is independently useful. Tier 1 ships today with zero dependencies — you don’t need Graphviz installed to use the knowledge layer.

Try It

Draw the system.
Find the bugs.

# Add to your bundle includes: - bundle: dot-graph:behaviors/dot-graph

The diagram isn’t the deliverable. The understanding is.
